title: neoholubia pubescens var. alpina
intro: Neoholubia pubescens var. alpina, also known as the alpine form of a species of flowering plant, is a type of succulent native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or consumed orally. However, more research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
